property_verdicts.verdict_explanation: FSM enforces mutual authentication through signature verification in both directions (Sigma2 and Sigma3)
All transitions involving certificate processing include validate_certificate_chain() action with trusted_rcac parameter
FSM includes validate_certificate_chain() with destination_id parameter which binds to Fabric ID; transitions to Validation_Failed on mismatch
FSM includes sign() and verify_signature() actions in Sigma2/Sigma3 transitions
FSM generates fresh ephemeral keypairs via generate_ecdh_keypair() in both initiator and responder paths
FSM uses allocate_unique_session_id() function with guard that ensures no overlap
FSM computes destination_id with IPK binding through compute_destination_identifier() function
FSM uses IPK for CASE operations only but doesn't explicitly prevent group communication use - enforcement is outside CASE FSM scope
FSM abstracts transport layer - reliability is assumed but not modeled in FSM transitions
FSM shows encode_tbsdata() followed by sign(), then encode_tbedata() with signature - TBS never in transmitted message
FSM includes encrypt_and_generate_aead() before send_message() for both Sigma2 and Sigma3
FSM uses generate_random(32) for initiator_random/responder_random and generate_random(16) for resumption_id
FSM derives session keys via derive_session_encryption_keys(shared_secret, ipk, transcript_hash) with correct parameters
FSM derives both i2r_key and r2i_key separately from session_keys struct
FSM derives attestation_challenge but usage restriction is outside CASE FSM scope
FSM includes initialize_local_message_counter() and initialize_message_reception_state() in session key transitions
FSM has explicit transition Idle->Validation_Failed with guard for XOR case of resumption fields
FSM uses validate_resumption_parameters() which requires cached SharedSecret and ResumptionID
FSM includes verify_resume2_mic() and AEAD verification in validate_resumption_parameters()
FSM includes set_session_timestamp(current_time()) in all Session_Keys_Established transitions
All error transitions include send_status_report(FAILURE, SECURE_CHANNEL, INVALID_PARAMETER) and cleanup_session_state()
FSM validates certificate chain with destination_id but explicit Node ID match check is implicit in validate_certificate_chain()
FSM computes transcript_hash := compute_hash(msg1 || msg2 || msg3) before session key derivation
Session ID and Security Flags are transport layer concerns not modeled in FSM
S Flag and DSIZ are message layer concerns not modeled in FSM
Exchange Flags are message layer concerns not modeled in FSM
Session count limits are implementation concerns not modeled in FSM state machine
FSM includes send_status_report(SUCCESS, SECURE_CHANNEL, SESSION_ESTABLISHMENT_SUCCESS) in send_sigma_finished transition
FSM only enables encryption_keys_active in Session_Active state after receive_sigma_finished/send_sigma_finished
FSM has explicit fallback_to_full_handshake transition with guard for resumption failure scenarios
FSM calls generate_ecdh_keypair() for each session in both initiator and responder paths
FSM includes certificate validation but DN encoding rules are implicit in validate_responder_noc/icac functions
FSM includes validate_responder_icac() which should check Fabric ID consistency per spec
FSM correctly derives S2K using derive_s2k(ipk, responder_random, responder_eph_pubkey, msg1, shared_secret)
Byte-identical encoding is implementation detail not modeled in FSM
TLV parsing behavior is implementation detail not modeled in FSM
FSM uses get_operational_group_key(fabric_id, second_newest_epoch_key) for IPK retrieval
FSM generates resumption_id := generate_random(16) in Sigma2_Generated transition
FSM shows encode_tbsdata includes both initiator_eph_pubkey and responder_eph_pubkey parameters
FSM uses distinct nonces sigma2_nonce and sigma3_nonce for different message types
Nonce format is implementation detail abstracted in FSM as sigma2_nonce/sigma3_nonce constants
FSM uses empty_aad parameter in all encrypt_and_generate_aead and decrypt_and_verify_aead calls
Node ID range check is implicit in certificate validation functions but not explicitly modeled
TLV encoding order is implementation detail handled by encode_sigma* functions
FSM includes Idle->BUSY_State transition with calculate_retry_delay() and little_endian_16bit(retry_time_ms)
FSM includes evict_session_lru transition with find_least_recently_used_session() action
Basic Constraints check is implicit in validate_certificate_chain() but not explicitly modeled
FSM uses second_newest_epoch_key parameter in get_operational_group_key() calls
FSM includes send_status_report_to_peer(lru_session, SESSION_EVICTED) before cleanup in eviction transition
Nonce construction details for session messages are outside CASE establishment FSM scope
FSM now includes verify_fabric_id_cross_check() action in Sigma3_Received transition comparing initiator_noc.fabric_id with matched_fabric.fabric_id
FSM now includes validate_ephemeral_key_on_curve() action before ECDH computation in both receive_sigma2 and receive_sigma3 transitions
FSM now includes validate_certificate_validity_period() action for NOC and ICAC in receive_sigma2 transition
FSM correctly shows S2K derivation using derive_s2k() with Msg1 hash via sigma1_msg parameter, and S3K derivation using derive_s3k() with Msg1||Msg2 via separate msg1 and msg2 parameters
Timeout behavior is not explicitly modeled in FSM - would require timer-based transitions
FSM now includes explicit Sigma1_Generated->Validation_Failed transition with guard resume2_mic_verification_failed and actions to send FAILURE status report
This is a specification awareness property, not a violation. FSM correctly reflects asymmetry: responder identity learned in Sigma2_Received, initiator identity learned in Sigma3_Received
This is a specification gap awareness property. FSM does not include revocation checks, accurately reflecting the specification which does not require real-time revocation checking

critical_transitions.why_property_holds: Initiator verifies responder's signature proving responder possesses NOC private key
Responder verifies initiator's signature proving initiator possesses NOC private key
Responder signs TBS data with NOC private key proving identity
Initiator signs TBS data with NOC private key proving identity

specification_evidence.quote: Prove possession of the NOC private key by signing the ephemeral keys and NOC (sigma-2-tbsdata and sigma-3-tbsdata)
specification_evidence.context: Description of basic CASE protocol objectives

specification_evidence.quote: Exchange certificates to prove identities (Sigma2.encrypted2.responderNOC and Sigma3.encrypted3.initiatorNOC)
specification_evidence.context: Description of certificate exchange for identity proof
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Confirms both parties must prove identity via certificates

supporting_assumptions.assumption: ECDSA signature scheme is secure and unforgeable
supporting_assumptions.where_enforced: FSM guards verify_signature() before state transition

supporting_assumptions.assumption: NOC private keys are securely stored and not exposed

critical_transitions.why_property_holds: Full certificate chain validation with trusted_rcac binding to destination_id
Responder validates initiator certificate chain against fabric trust anchor
Failed certificate validation transitions to error state

specification_evidence.quote: The certificate chain SHALL chain back to the Trusted Root CA Certificate TrustedRCAC whose public key was used in the computation of the Destination Identifier when generating Sigma1.
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Explicitly requires chain back to TrustedRCAC

specification_evidence.quote: All the elements in the certificate chain SHALL respect the Matter Certificate DN Encoding Rules, including range checks for identifiers such as Fabric ID and Node ID.

supporting_assumptions.assumption: TrustedRCAC is securely provisioned during commissioning

critical_transitions.guard_condition: (msg1.resumption_id != null && msg1.initiator_resume_mic == null) || (msg1.resumption_id == null && msg1.initiator_resume_mic != null)
critical_transitions.why_property_holds: XOR condition explicitly catches mismatched resumption fields and sends INVALID_PARAMETER

critical_transitions.guard_condition: (msg1.resumption_id != null && msg1.initiator_resume_mic != null) || (msg1.resumption_id == null && msg1.initiator_resume_mic == null)
critical_transitions.why_property_holds: Only valid combinations (both present OR both absent) proceed to normal processing

specification_evidence.quote: If Msg1 contains either a resumptionID or an initiatorResumeMIC field but not both, the responder SHALL send a status report: StatusReport(General-Code: FAILURE, ProtocolId: SECURE_CHANNEL, ProtocolCode: INVALID_PARAMETER) and perform no further processing.
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Explicitly requires both fields or neither

critical_transitions.guard_condition: resumption_mode == true && (resumption_id_not_found == true || shared_secret_unavailable == true || resume_mic_verification_failed == true)
critical_transitions.why_property_holds: Explicit fallback transition when resumption fails for any reason

specification_evidence.quote: If the value of Success is FALSE, the responder SHALL continue processing Sigma1 as if it didn't include any resumption information by continuing the steps in Section 4.14.2.3.5, 'Validate Sigma1 Destination ID'.
specification_evidence.source: Section 4.14.2.3.10, 'Validate Sigma1 with Resumption', Page 184, Item 4
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Explicitly requires fallback to full handshake on resumption failure

critical_transitions.why_property_holds: TBS data explicitly includes both ephemeral public keys before signing
TBS data explicitly includes both ephemeral public keys before signing

specification_evidence.quote: sigma-2-tbsdata => STRUCTURE [ tag-order ] { responderNOC [1] : OCTET STRING, responderICAC [2, optional] : OCTET STRING, responderEphPubKey [3] : ec-pub-key, initiatorEphPubKey [4] : ec-pub-key }
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Shows both ephemeral keys are included in signed data

specification_evidence.quote: sigma-3-tbsdata => STRUCTURE [ tag-order ] { initiatorNOC [1] : OCTET STRING, initiatorICAC [2, optional] : OCTET STRING, initiatorEphPubKey [3] : ec-pub-key, responderEphPubKey [4] : ec-pub-key }
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Shows both ephemeral keys are included in signed data

critical_transitions.why_property_holds: IPK used for Destination ID computation

actions: s2k_key := derive_s2k(ipk, responder_random, responder_eph_keypair.public_key, msg1, shared_secret)
critical_transitions.why_property_holds: IPK used for S2K key derivation

fsm_analysis.partial_hold_reason: FSM only models CASE usage of IPK. Enforcement of exclusive use (preventing group communication use) is outside CASE FSM scope - it's a Group Key Management Cluster responsibility.

specification_evidence.quote: The IPK is exclusively used for CASE and SHALL NOT be used for operational group communication.

specification_gap.what_spec_claims: IPK SHALL NOT be used for operational group communication
specification_gap.what_spec_fails_to_specify: How this restriction is enforced at the protocol level. The Group Key Management Cluster manages both IPK and operational group keys, but the separation is based on key ID conventions rather than protocol-level enforcement.
specification_gap.consequence: Implementation error could accidentally use IPK for group encryption, potentially leaking it to more nodes than intended
specification_gap.security_impact: Leaked IPK would allow outsiders to compute valid Destination IDs, enabling presence probing and targeted attacks

supporting_assumptions.assumption: Group Key Management Cluster correctly separates IPK from operational keys

gaps_identified.description: IPK exclusive use is stated but enforcement mechanism not specified in CASE protocol

critical_transitions.observation: destination_id passed to validate_certificate_chain() but explicit Node ID extraction and comparison not shown

fsm_analysis.partial_hold_reason: The FSM passes destination_id to validate_certificate_chain(), which should contain the expected Node ID. However, the explicit action of extracting Node ID from NOC and comparing to Destination ID computation is implicit in the validate_certificate_chain function, not explicitly modeled.

specification_evidence.quote: The Fabric ID and Node ID SHALL match the intended identity of the receiver Node, as included in the computation of the Destination Identifier when generating Sigma1.
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Confirms Node ID match requirement exists

specification_gap.what_spec_claims: Node ID in NOC SHALL match intended identity in Destination Identifier
specification_gap.what_spec_fails_to_specify: The exact algorithm for extracting Node ID from Destination ID computation and comparing to NOC Node ID. Destination ID is a hash - how does receiver extract Node ID for comparison?
specification_gap.consequence: The receiver already knows the target Node ID (used to compute Destination ID), so it compares received NOC Node ID against its own knowledge, not against Destination ID directly
specification_gap.security_impact: If implementation incorrectly validates, attacker could substitute NOC for different node

gaps_identified.description: Node ID comparison is implicit in validate_certificate_chain() but actual comparison logic not explicitly modeled

critical_transitions.observation: NOC validation function called but specific range check not explicitly modeled

fsm_analysis.partial_hold_reason: FSM calls validate_responder_noc() but doesn't explicitly show Operational Node ID range check. The spec requires range validation but the FSM abstracts this into the validation function.

specification_evidence.quote: All the elements in the certificate chain SHALL respect the Matter Certificate DN Encoding Rules, including range checks for identifiers such as Fabric ID and Node ID.

gaps_identified.description: Node ID range validation is required by spec but abstracted into validate_*_noc functions in FSM

critical_transitions.observation: ICAC validation function called but Basic Constraints check not explicitly modeled

fsm_analysis.partial_hold_reason: FSM includes validate_responder_icac() and validate_certificate_chain() but doesn't explicitly show Basic Constraints extension validation. This is abstracted into the validation functions.

specification_evidence.quote: If an ICAC is present, and it contains a Fabric ID in its subject, then it SHALL match the FabricID in the NOC leaf certificate.
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Confirms ICAC validation is required but doesn't explicitly mention Basic Constraints in CASE section

specification_gap.what_spec_fails_to_specify: Explicit Basic Constraints validation requirement in CASE section (may be specified in certificate format section)
specification_gap.consequence: ICAC without proper Basic Constraints could be accepted
specification_gap.security_impact: Leaf certificate could be used as intermediate CA

gaps_identified.description: Basic Constraints validation likely specified in certificate format section, not explicitly in CASE section

key_findings.finding: CASE protocol FSM correctly enforces mutual authentication through signature verification in both directions
key_findings.evidence: Transitions Sigma2_Received and Sigma3_Received both include verify_signature() actions

key_findings.finding: Certificate chain validation is properly modeled with binding to Destination ID/Fabric
key_findings.evidence: validate_certificate_chain() called with trusted_rcac and destination_id parameters

key_findings.finding: Resumption validation correctly handles XOR case for resumption fields
key_findings.evidence: Explicit guard condition catches mismatched fields and transitions to Validation_Failed

key_findings.finding: Session key derivation correctly includes transcript hash binding
key_findings.evidence: derive_session_encryption_keys(shared_secret, ipk, transcript_hash) uses all three messages

key_findings.finding: Some low-level protocol details (message flags, TLV encoding, nonce format) are abstracted and not verifiable in FSM
key_findings.evidence: 10 properties marked UNVERIFIABLE due to abstraction level

key_findings.finding: IPK exclusive use enforcement is outside CASE FSM scope - potential gap
key_findings.evidence: FSM only models CASE usage, prevention of group comm use not modeled

key_findings.finding: NEW: Sigma3 Fabric ID cross-check now explicitly modeled in FSM
key_findings.evidence: Added verify_fabric_id_cross_check() action in Sigma3_Received transition per Page 183 requirement

key_findings.finding: NEW: Ephemeral public key curve validation now explicitly modeled
key_findings.evidence: Added validate_ephemeral_key_on_curve() action before ECDH computation to prevent invalid curve attacks

key_findings.finding: NEW: Certificate validity period check now explicitly modeled
key_findings.evidence: Added validate_certificate_validity_period() action for NOC and ICAC certificates

key_findings.finding: NEW: Resume2MIC validation failure handling at initiator side now explicitly modeled
key_findings.evidence: Added Sigma1_Generated->Validation_Failed transition for resume2_mic_verification_failed scenario

key_findings.finding: SPECIFICATION GAP IDENTIFIED: No certificate revocation during CASE
key_findings.evidence: Specification does not require real-time certificate revocation checking during session establishment
key_findings.security_impact: MEDIUM - Revoked certificates may be used until next certificate chain update

key_findings.finding: SPECIFICATION AWARENESS: Mutual authentication asymmetry exists by design
key_findings.evidence: Initiator learns responder identity in Sigma2, responder learns initiator identity in Sigma3 - could be exploited for probing
key_findings.security_impact: LOW - Design trade-off, not a vulnerability

key_findings.evidence: Deep analysis of core_spec pages 171-194 found no protocol-level security vulnerabilities. All potential issues have mitigations via SHALL requirements.

recommendations.recommendation: Add explicit Node ID extraction and comparison action in certificate validation
Add explicit Basic Constraints validation action for ICAC
Document IPK exclusive use enforcement mechanism in FSM or separate Group Key FSM
Consider adding message layer FSM to verify flag/encoding properties
Add timer-based transitions for session establishment timeout
Consider specification enhancement to add optional certificate revocation checking during CASE

gaps_detail.description: No certificate revocation checking during CASE session establishment
gaps_detail.rationale: Specification relies on fabric-level certificate management rather than real-time revocation

gaps_detail.description: IPK epoch key transition window during key rotation
gaps_detail.mitigation_in_spec: Uses second-newest epoch key for generation, validates both during reception
gaps_detail.rationale: Specification provides mitigation via 'second-newest' selection and multi-key validation

gaps_detail.description: Authentication asymmetry allows initiator probing of responder identity
gaps_detail.mitigation_in_spec: Identity protection via IPK and Destination Identifier
gaps_detail.rationale: Design trade-off for efficiency; initiator cannot probe without knowing IPK and target Destination ID

specification_vulnerability_analysis.conclusion: No specification-level vulnerabilities found in CASE protocol. The protocol correctly implements SIGMA-based authenticated key exchange with identity protection. All identified gaps have mitigations specified via SHALL requirements or are design trade-offs with acceptable security impact.
